label: MK
stem: According to the ASA/SCA practice guidelines, in which setting is TEE explicitly recommended to be used in all cases (absent contraindication)?
options: All open-heart and thoracic aortic surgical procedures
All elective outpatient procedures under sedation
Only when the patient is symptomatic preoperatively
Only when transthoracic echocardiography is unavailable
explanation: The ASA/SCA guideline states TEE should be used in all open-heart (e.g., valvular procedures) and thoracic aortic surgical procedures, and should be considered in CABG as well.

label: MK
stem: A sonographer notices the LV apex appears unusually short and rounded in the ME four-chamber view. What is the most likely explanation?
options: Normal anatomic variant requiring no action
explanation: Foreshortening of the LV apex is a common acquisition error in the ME four-chamber view. The probe should be withdrawn or adjusted to capture the true apex, or LV size and function will be underestimated.

label: MK
stem: Per the BSE 2021 mitral valve disease guideline, what 3D vena contracta area threshold is used to define severe mitral regurgitation?
explanation: The BSE guideline defines the threshold for severe MR by 3D vena contracta area as greater than 40 mm-squared.

label: MK
stem: A patient has an aortic valve Vmax of 4.3 m/s and a mean gradient of 38 mmHg. What is the most appropriate severity classification?
explanation: Vmax 4.0-4.9 m/s alone is sufficient to classify severe AS per the BSE 2021 grading table, even though the mean gradient (38 mmHg) falls just short of the 40 mmHg severe threshold — either criterion alone is sufficient.

label: ADV
stem: Per the 2021 BSE guideline, where should the LVOT diameter be measured for the continuity equation, and why does this matter?
options: 1 cm below the cusp insertion point — unrelated to accuracy
At the point of cusp insertion — improves inter-observer reproducibility and better corresponds to invasively-derived AVA
At the sinotubular junction — simplifies measurement
Anywhere convenient, since location does not affect the calculated AVA
explanation: The 2021 BSE guideline changed recommended practice to measure the LVOT diameter at the point of aortic cusp insertion (not 1 cm below, as in older practice), improving reproducibility and correlation with invasively-measured AVA.
